summary: A Russian FPV drone entered a Ukrainian Armed Forces pickup truck through an open window but failed to detonate. The Kalashnikov Concern began preliminary testing of the Krona air defense system for countering UAVs under combat-like conditions, integrating detection, control, and mobile or stationary missile modules. In the Sumy region, an airstrike hit a Ukrainian air defense system in Krasnopolye, and a loitering munition struck a Ukrainian position in Pavlovka. Operators of the "Rubicon" Center reported destroying 193 targets on April 14, 2026, including Ukrainian fixed-wing UAVs and personnel across multiple directions. The company XDOWN unveiled the Small Tactical Unmanned Drone (STUD), a 2.7 kg, 300 km/h portable FPV drone with an 800-gram warhead and 64 km range, featuring a foldable Switchblade-like design. Ukrainian soldiers from the 36th Marine Brigade survived a Russian FPV drone attack in the Kostiantynivka direction, with a drone detector visible at 0:11 in the footage. The 238th Guards Artillery Brigade intercepted a Ukrainian "Hornet" UAV using the "Yolka" anti-aircraft drone, causing its warhead to detonate, while another kinetic intercept triggered the parachute system of a Ukrainian "Shark-M" UAV, which was captured by the 16th Special Purpose Brigade in the Krasny Liman direction.
